text: The robotic instruments are exchanged for a robotic needle driver, while the ProGrasp or fenestrated bipolar instruments remain in the left hand, potentially used as a needle driver depending on the surgeon's preference.

The surgeon uses a 3-0 monocryl suture on an SH needle to reconstruct the inner layer of the resection bed. The suture is passed through the renal capsule into the defect, and anchored with a knot and a hemlock clip on the renal capsule.

The surgeon uses continuous suturing to close small vessels and repair entries into the collecting system within the resection bed, employing a robotic needle driver. Various suture options, including forovicral and barb sutures, are available depending on surgeon preference.
The surgeon carefully places sutures in the resection bed, ensuring they are not too deep to prevent vascular injury and avoid affecting the collecting system or creating a pseudoaneurysm.

The surgeon passes the needle from the inner layer to the outside of the kidney through the renal capsule. An assistant places a hemlock clip to secure the stitch, and additional sutures are applied to occlude any small bleeding vessels.
The surgeon focuses on reconstructing the inner layer of the kidney to achieve hemostasis during renorrhaphy, using sutures to secure the tissue and control bleeding.
